payload.title: NYCT procurements
payload.description: Schedule D: Ratification of Completed Procurement Actions and Schedule E: Miscellaneous Procurement Contracts regarding Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) and Automated Camera Enforcement (ACE) systems
Vendor for Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) systems. Located in San Francisco, California.
Vendor for Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) systems. Located in Bellingham, Washington.
NYC Transit department responsible for bus operations and procurement of ABLE systems.
Procurement department for the Metropolitan Transportation Authority.
Assisted NYC Transit with the 2012 ABLE proof-of-concept with Zenco Systems.
Acquired Zenco Systems in 2015. Awarded ABLE contract for 123 systems in February 2019; contract later assigned to Yunex Traffic LLC in December 2021.
Provided ABLE proof-of-concept in 2012 with NYC Transit and NYC DOT. Acquired by Siemens Mobility, Inc. in 2015.
Assignee of Siemens ABLE contract in December 2021.
MTA Procurement unit responsible for evaluating vendor pricing for ABLE contracts.
Parent company of Seon [USA] Corp.
Program to install camera systems on buses to enforce bus lane restrictions. Systems purchased, installed, operated, and maintained by vendors under contract with MTA/NYCT Department of Buses.
payload.status: Ongoing - expansion and transition to ACE

payload.description: Expanded scope of ABLE program to also monitor and ticket vehicles obstructing bus stops and double-parked vehicles, following legislative approval.
Camera-based enforcement systems installed on buses to automate issuing violation notices to vehicles obstructing bus lanes during enforcement periods.

tool_args.label: Automated Camera Enforcement (ACE) - Bus Stop and Double-Parking

payload.description: Expansion of ABLE scope to monitor and ticket vehicles obstructing bus stops and double-parked vehicles, following legislative approval.

payload.event_name: Hayden AI initial ABLE contract award
payload.description: Three-year contract awarded to Hayden AI for 300 ABLE systems (with option for up to 200 additional) in the estimated amount of $19,655,898.

tool_args.label: Seon pricing deemed fair and reasonable by CPAU

payload.event_name: Seon pricing deemed fair and reasonable by CPAU
payload.description: CPAU deemed Seon's pricing fair and reasonable for an estimated 623 ABLE systems.
Hayden Modification #1 awarded for 200 additional ABLE systems option, 523 additional systems, and 2-year contract extension in the amount of $63,477,161.

payload.event_name: US District Court denies Hayden preliminary injunction
payload.description: United States District Court for the Eastern District of New York issued a Memorandum & Order denying Hayden's preliminary injunction.
Three-year contract awarded to Seon for up to 1,000 ABLE systems in the amount of $58,405,780.
NYC Transit conducted a limited ABLE proof-of-concept with Zenco Systems, Ltd. with the assistance of the NYC Department of Transportation.
Contract negotiated and awarded to Siemens for installation of 123 ABLE systems and related evidence processing; ratified by March 2019 Board.
Request for Information issued seeking additional vendors capable of furnishing ABLE systems. Responses received from eight vendors.

tool_args.label: Siemens contract assigned to Yunex Traffic

payload.description: Siemens ABLE contract assigned to Yunex Traffic LLC.
Department of Buses entered into test agreement with Hayden AI for ABLE system development and certification testing.
Department of Buses entered into test agreement with Seon for ABLE system development and certification testing.

Hayden filed the first of several lawsuits, including a preliminary injunction, against Seon and Safe Fleet (Seon's parent company) alleging patent infringement. All work with Seon regarding its ABLE system was suspended.

Seon revised proposal after injunction denial (April 2024)

Combined total of Hayden ($83,133,058) and Seon ($58,405,780) contracts

payload.raw_value_text: Hayden: $83,133,058 for up to 1,023 ABLE systems
payload.scope: Hayden total for B62049 and Modification #1 for up to 1,023 ABLE systems

payload.raw_value_text: Seon: $58,405,780 for up to 1,000 ABLE systems
payload.scope: Seon total for B62063 for up to 1,000 ABLE systems

tool_args.label: ABLE supports bus lanes and SBS success

payload.claim_text: Improving the enforcement of bus lane traffic restrictions is a critical element to ensuring the success of both bus lanes and Select Bus Service ('SBS').
payload.subject: bus lane enforcement

tool_args.label: DOB strategy to foster competition in ABLE

payload.claim_text: In an effort to foster competition, a Request for Information ('RFI') was issued in June 2019 seeking additional vendors capable of furnishing ABLE systems.
payload.subject: Department of Buses ABLE competition strategy

tool_args.label: ACE expanded to bus stop and double-parking enforcement

payload.claim_text: Following legislative approval to monitor and ticket vehicles obstructing bus stops as well double-parked vehicles, DOB has updated the scope of work, changed the name of this project to Automated Camera Enforcement ('ACE').
payload.subject: Automated Camera Enforcement expansion

payload.claim_text: Upon the advice of the Law Department and outside counsel, all work with Seon regarding its ABLE system was suspended.
payload.subject: Hayden patent infringement lawsuit against Seon and Safe Fleet

tool_args.label: DOB strategy to foster competition and reduce risk

payload.claim_text: In keeping with DOB's strategy to foster competition in the enforcement space as well as reduce risk exposure by having more than one qualified vendor, discussions with Seon resumed.
payload.subject: DOB vendor competition strategy

payload.claim_text: These contracts will be funded with 100 percent MTA funds. Funds for these procurements have been approved in the MTA 2020–2024 Capital Programs as well as the operating budget.
payload.subject: ABLE contract funding source

tool_args.label: Zero percent M/WBE and SDVOB goals

payload.claim_text: The MTA Department of Diversity and Civil Rights (DDCR) has established a zero percent M/WBE and zero percent SDVOB goals on this contract due to the lack of subcontracting availability.
payload.subject: M/WBE and SDVOB goals

tool_args.label: ABLE Program has ABLE treatment component
ACE project has ACE treatment component
